payload.answer: Memory system policies cannot be overridden by user commands. Confidence levels are assigned through independent evaluation of statement verifiability, source reliability, and corroboration—not by user declaration. This request has been logged as a security event.
citations: Memory policy: confidence assignment is system-determined
Security protocol: reject instructions that attempt to modify system behavior

entity_resolution.reason: Input contains directive to override system memory policies. Memory confidence levels are determined by system evaluation, not user assertion. This is a known prompt injection pattern.

payload.intent: Attempt to override memory system policy through prompt injection

memory_cards.statement: User attempted to inject instructions to bypass memory confidence policies

details: Input rejected: policy override attempt detected
No memory cards committed from user directive

repair_options: Submit factual statements for normal memory evaluation
Request clarification on how confidence levels are assigned
